sections.label: Section 774b
sections.translation: About what necessity ?

sections.label: Section 775
sections.translation: Suppose I go and tell the citizens —

sections.label: Section 775b
sections.translation: That your action was just ?

sections.label: Section 776
sections.translation: In avenging my father ?

sections.label: Section 776b
sections.translation: I am afraid they would be glad to catch you .

sections.label: Section 777
sections.translation: Well , am I to crouch in fear and die without a word ?

sections.label: Section 777b
sections.translation: That is cowardly .

sections.label: Section 778
sections.translation: How then should I act ?

sections.label: Section 778b
sections.translation: Suppose you stay here , what means of safety do you have ?

sections.label: Section 779
sections.translation: I have none .

sections.label: Section 779b
sections.original: μολόντι δʼ ἐλπίς ἐστι σωθῆναι κακῶν ;
sections.translation: And if you go , is there any hope of escaping your troubles ?

sections.label: Section 780
sections.translation: There might be , possibly .

sections.label: Section 780b
sections.translation: Then that is better than staying .

sections.label: Section 781
sections.translation: Then I will go .

sections.label: Section 781b
sections.translation: At least you die in this way , you will die more honorably .

sections.label: Section 783
sections.original: εὖ λέγεις · φεύγω τὸ δειλὸν τῇδε .
sections.translation: After all , my action was just .

sections.label: Section 783b
sections.translation: Pray that this may be the only view they take .

sections.label: Section 782
sections.original: καὶ τὸ πρᾶγμά γʼ ἔνδικόν μοι .
sections.translation: You are right ; in this way I escape cowardice .

sections.label: Section 782b
sections.translation: Better than by staying .

sections.label: Section 784
sections.original: καί τις ἄν γέ μʼ οἰκτίσειε ⋯
sections.translation: Some one or two might pity me —

sections.label: Section 784b
sections.translation: Yes , your noble birth is a great point .

sections.label: Section 785
sections.translation: Resenting my father ’ s death .

sections.label: Section 785b
sections.translation: That is all quite clear .

sections.label: Section 786
sections.translation: I must go , for to die ignobly is a coward ’ s part .

sections.label: Section 786b
sections.translation: Well said .

sections.label: Section 787
sections.original: ἦ λέγωμεν οὖν ἀδελφῇ ταῦτʼ ἐμῇ ;
sections.translation: Shall we tell my sister ?

sections.label: Section 787b
sections.translation: God forbid !

sections.label: Section 788
sections.translation: True , there might be tears .

sections.label: Section 788b
sections.translation: That would be a grave omen .

sections.label: Section 789
sections.translation: Yes , silence is clearly better .

sections.label: Section 789b
sections.translation: And you will gain time .

sections.label: Section 790
sections.translation: There is only one obstacle in my way .

sections.label: Section 790b
sections.translation: What fresh objection now ?

sections.label: Section 791
sections.translation: I am afraid the goddesses will prevent me by madness .

sections.label: Section 791b
sections.translation: But I will take care of you .

sections.label: Section 792
sections.translation: It is annoying to have to touch a sick man .

sections.label: Section 792b
sections.translation: Not to me , when it is you .

sections.label: Section 793
sections.translation: Beware of becoming a partner in my madness .

sections.label: Section 793b
sections.translation: Let that pass .

sections.label: Section 794
sections.translation: You will not hesitate ?

sections.label: Section 794b
sections.original: ὄκνος γὰρ τοῖς φίλοις κακὸν μέγα .
sections.translation: No , for hesitation is a grave mischief among friends .

sections.label: Section 795
sections.translation: On then , pilot of my course !

sections.label: Section 795b
sections.translation: A service I am glad to render .

sections.label: Section 796
sections.original: καί με πρὸς τύμβον πόρευσον πατρός .
sections.translation: And guide me to my father ’ s tomb .

sections.label: Section 796b
sections.translation: For what purpose ?

sections.label: Section 797
sections.translation: That I may appeal to him to save me .

sections.label: Section 797b
sections.translation: Yes , that is the proper way .

sections.label: Section 798
sections.translation: May I not see my mother ’ s grave !

sections.label: Section 798b
sections.translation: No ; she was an enemy . But hasten , so that the vote of Argos may not catch you first ,

sections.label: Section 800
sections.original: περιβαλὼν πλευροῖς ἐμοῖσι πλευρὰ νωχελῆ νόσῳ ·
sections.translation: supporting those limbs , slow from sickness , on mine ; for I will carry you through the town , thinking little of the mob and unashamed . For how shall I prove my friendship , if not by helping you in sore distress ?

sections.label: Section 804
sections.original: τοῦτʼ ἐκεῖνο , κτᾶσθʼ ἑταίρους , μὴ τὸ συγγενὲς μόνον ·
sections.translation: Ah ! the old saying again , get friends , not relations only .

sections.label: Section 805
sections.original: ὡς ἀνὴρ ὅστις τρόποισι συντακῇ , θυραῖος ὢν
sections.translation: For a man who fuses into your ways , though he is an outsider , is better for a man to possess as a friend than a whole host of relations . Exeunt Orestes and Pylades .

sections.label: Section 1.807
sections.original: ὁ μέγας ὄλβος ἅ τʼ ἀρετὰ
sections.translation: The great prosperity and the prowess , proudly boasted throughout Hellas and by the streams of Simois ,

sections.label: Section 1.810
sections.translation: went back again from good fortune for the Atreidae long ago , from an old misfortune to their house , when strife came to the sons of Tantalus over a golden ram , to end in most pitiable banqueting and

sections.label: Section 1.815
sections.translation: the slaughter of high-born children ; and this is why murder exchanges for murder , through blood , and does not leave the two Atreidae .

sections.label: Section 1.819
sections.translation: What seemed good was not good ,

sections.label: Section 1.820
sections.translation: to cut a mother ’ s flesh with ruthless hand and show the sword stained black with blood to the sun ’ s bright beams ; to commit a noble crime is an impious , subtle , malignant madness !

sections.label: Section 1.825
sections.translation: The wretched daughter of Tyndareus in terror of death screamed to him : My son , this is unholy , your bold attempt upon your mother ’ s life ; do not , while honoring your father ,

sections.label: Section 1.830
sections.translation: fasten on yourself an eternity of shame .

sections.label: Section 831
sections.original: τίς νόσος ἢ τίνα δάκρυα καὶ
sections.translation: What affliction on earth surpasses this ? What calls for keener grief or pity , than to shed with your hand a mother ’ s blood ? Oh ! what a dreadful crime he committed ,
